title: ranunculus aquatilis subsp. trichophyllus
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis subsp. trichophyllus, also known as white water crowfoot, is a subspecies of the water crowfoot plant, characterized by its delicate white flowers and submerged leaves. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus aquatilis subsp. trichophyllus include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antiviral and antibacterial activities. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although its safety and efficacy should be further studied.
